title: Real-time telemetry acquisition
description: The system shall acquire and record position, velocity, acceleration, and body attitude data at a minimum rate of 10 Hz during active flight.

title: Trajectory reconstruction
description: The system shall compute and display a 3D trajectory reconstruction from raw telemetry, including downrange distance, crossrange deviation, and altitude as a function of time.
source: Position as a function of time

title: Performance characteristic computation
description: The system shall derive and report performance characteristics including maximum range, peak speed, time of flight, maximum altitude, and apogee time.
source: Range, speed, time of flight, altitude

title: Anomaly detection and alerting
description: The system shall detect and flag anomalous telemetry conditions including altitude threshold breaches, excessive dynamic pressure, attitude deviations exceeding 15 degrees, and speed limit violations.

title: Test suite execution
description: The system shall execute a configurable suite of automated validation tests against telemetry data and report pass/fail status per test case with actual versus expected values.

title: Requirements traceability matrix
description: The system shall maintain a bidirectional requirements traceability matrix linking each software requirement to one or more test cases, with live verification status.

title: ReqIF export
description: The system shall export the requirements matrix in ReqIF 1.0 XML format compatible with IBM DOORS and Rational Publishing Engine.

title: Test report generation
description: The system shall generate a PDF test report containing flight summary statistics, anomaly log, test suite pass/fail table, and requirements coverage percentage.

title: Mach number computation
description: The system shall compute Mach number at each telemetry frame using standard atmosphere model with altitude-dependent speed of sound.

title: Dynamic pressure monitoring
description: The system should compute and monitor dynamic pressure (q-bar) throughout flight and alert when structural load limits are approached.
